description: §65.3.2 / §65.6 / §65.10 — a `<theme>` variant (`.Dark`) re-binds `ghost`, but no base token `ghost` is declared. Per §65.6 a variant re-binds a SUBSET of the base tokens (the base holds the default-state value); a variant-only token has no value in the default state, so `color: ghost` would resolve to nothing there. The token lowering (emit-css.ts / emit-theme-reset.ts `emitThemeCss`) fires E-THEME-TOKEN-UNKNOWN — the DECIDABLE core of §65.3.2's use-site rule.
rationale: §65.10: E-THEME-TOKEN-UNKNOWN — a token reference that resolves to no in-scope base `<theme>`. The decidable, false-positive-free firing is a variant / `@media` auto-bind sub-block re-binding a name absent from the base token set (`ghost` here). This proves the emission-half validates the theme token structure end-to-end. Partitions into result.errors (E- prefix).
